chapter.title: Dependencies injection
chapter.simpara: QALIPSIS relies on the dependency injection framework from Micronaut and supports the injection of dependencies in the constructors of the scenario declaring classes as well as in the methods annotated with @Scenario.

warning.simpara: Ensure you have the following dependency in the project where your beans or factories are compiled: kapt("io.qalipsis:api-processors:0.2.0-SNAPSHOT")

section.title: Injection of a singleton bean
section.simpara: You can declare a singleton bean using the annotation @jakarta.inject.Singleton on a type, or use a Micronaut factory.

callout.para: The annotation @jakarta.inject.Singleton can be added to a class to automatically create a bean and declare it as singleton
The annotation @jakarta.inject.Singleton added to a method declares the returned bean as a singleton
The retry policy is automatically injected into the factory method

section.simpara: Once beans are created, you can inject them in the constructor or the scenario method:

callout.para: The previously created bean of type RetryPolicy is injected in the constructor of the scenario declaring class.
The previously created bean of type Executor is injected in the scenario declaring method.

section.title: Qualifying the bean to inject
section.simpara: When several beans exist for the same type, you might want to select the one to inject. QALIPSIS supports named injection:

callout.para: When the constructor parameter is a property, precise the target of the annotation.
The annotation @Named selects the bean of type Executor qualified by the name "simple" for the injection.

section.simpara: While QALIPSIS only supports name qualifiers, you can read more about other qualifiers on Micronaut documentation.

section.title: Inject a property value
section.simpara: Properties for the runtime property sources are also injectable in scenario declaring classes constructors and methods.
The default value set in orElse is optional.
More about the property sources on Micronaut documentation.

section.title: Injectable container types
section.simpara: QALIPSIS supports the injection of a single instance of beans as shown above, as well as an Iterable or any of its subtypes - Collection, List, Set and their derivatives and Optional - also for properties.

section.title: Summary
section.simpara: The following sample of code shows all the supported methods of injection for beans and properties.
The example only shows for a scenario method, but this can only be used for the constructor of a class declaring scenarios.

callout.para: An instance of ClassToInject is injected, failing if missing
An instance of Optional<OtherClassToInject> is injected, replaced by an empty optional if missing
An instance of InterfaceToInject qualified by the name myInjectable is injected, failing if missing
A List of all the instances of classes inheriting from InterfaceToInject is injected
A List of all the instances of classes inheriting from InterfaceToInject and qualified by the name myInjectable is injected
The value of the property this-is-a-test is injected after its conversion to a Duration, failing if missing
The value of the property this-is-another-test is injected after its conversion to a Int, replace by 10 as Int if missing
The value of the property this-is-yet-another-test is injected after its conversion to a String, replaced by an empty optional if missing
